method.identity_policy: Segments without an enabled listener-recategorization rule remain in the same broad source class in both conditions.
method.substitution_policy: Equal-token substitutions can use the shared-state/common-RNG Kokoro path.
method.insertion_policy: Perceptual insertions require the separately controlled latent-slot path and are not silently approximated as substitutions.
method.prosody_policy: Prosodic rules are applied only to the structures and directions supported by their cited perception task; other contours remain paired controls.

sources.title: The perception and production of English interdental fricatives by Brazilian EFL learners
Where do illusory vowels come from?
Predicting Epenthetic Vowel Quality from Acoustics
The development of coda perception in second language phonology: A variationist perspective
The L2 perception of initial English /h/ and /r/ by Brazilian Portuguese learners of English
Investigating sources of phonological rarity and instability: a study of the palatal lateral approximant in Brazilian Portuguese
Unraveling Foreign Accent Prosody: Production and Perception of Lexical Stress in English by Brazilian Portuguese Speakers
Examining L1 Brazilian Portuguese speakers' sensitivity to English nuclear stress assignment
Intonational interference in English-L2 Brazilian speakers: production and perception

profiles.claim: Experimental English-through-Brazilian-Portuguese listener profile with broad vowel coverage, consonant substitutions, explicit insertion eligibility, and structure-bounded prosody evidence.

consonant_rules.note: Reis found /t/ was the most common /theta/ replacement and interpreted the perception results as /theta/ assimilation to BP /t/; the broad positional use remains an explicit extrapolation.
Reis reported /d/ in 94% of voiced-interdental replacements across the production tests and interpreted the perception pattern as BP /d/ assimilation.

insertion_rules.note: BP listeners show perceptual epenthesis, often /i/, but coarticulatory cues can override the default quality; the engine must not insert /i/ indiscriminately.

prosody_rules.note: The cited task found an initial-stress response bias when an initial secondary stress competed with final primary stress; this is not a license to move stress in every word.

profiles.claim: Experimental Brazilian-Portuguese-through-American-English listener profile with broad vowel coverage, consonant substitutions, and structure-bounded question-contour recategorization.

consonant_rules.note: American-English listeners frequently identified the BP palatal lateral as a palatal approximant. Misaki/Kokoro represents the source as /lj/; /jj/ is an equal-token renderer projection requiring acoustic and listener QC.
Coverage rule derived from inventory and acoustic proximity; no direct American-English listener mapping result is claimed.
Wong establishes poorer English-listener identification for palatal nasal categories, but not a universal /n/ response; /n/ is a labeled nearest-category projection.
The target is Kokoro's American-English alveolar-flap symbol; category correspondence is derived and must be checked for an audible non-artifactual effect.

prosody_rules.note: In the cited small study, English-L1 listeners preferred 'statement' for delexicalized BP rise-fall questions; this supports a bounded contour-category candidate, not a general intonation model.
